description: This pathway provides an overview of TGFB1 signalling on dNK cells with downstream effects of phenotypic conversion, suppressed cytotoxicity, and stimulation of processes such as trophoblast invasion and spiral artery remodeling as a result of the signalling. Within the dNK cells an inhibitory feedback loop of the TGF-B signalling is also occurring as a result of the protein SMAD7 binding to TGFBR1 preventing its phosphorylation, and SMURF2 which catalyses the ubiquitination of TGFBR1. Following activation of TGFBR2, TGFBR1 is phosphorylated and triggers the phosphorylation of SMAD2 and SMAD3 which bind to SMAD4 forming a complex following the phosphorylation. The SMAD2/3:4 complex then translocates to the nucleus where work is done to suppress the activation of proteins FCGR3A (aka CD16), IFNG, and NKG2D which usually give NK cells their cytotoxic phenotype.  The complex also works to activate transcription of genes translating CD103, CD49A, KIR2DL4, and CD9 which work to promote a tolerogenic phenotypic conversion/differentiation of NK cells within the decidua from CD16+ to CD16-. The suppressed cytotoxicity triggered by the complex allows for the activation of cytokines  such as IL8, CXCL10 which stimulate trophoblast invasion and factors such as VEGFC, PLGF, ANGPT1, and ANGPT2 which stimulate the spiral artery remodelling.
title: TGF-β1/SMAD2/3 signalling in decidual NK cells at the fetal-maternal interface
